title: Chewy Chocolate Chip Cookies
description: Perfectly chewy in the middle, slightly crispy on the edges, and loaded with chocolate chips. The ultimate cookie.

steps.instruction: Whisk flour, baking soda, and salt in a bowl. Set aside.
Beat butter and both sugars together with a mixer on medium speed for 3–4 minutes until light and fluffy.
Beat in eggs one at a time, then add vanilla.
Reduce speed to low and mix in the flour mixture just until combined. Fold in chocolate chips.
Cover dough and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes (up to 72 hours — longer = better flavor).
Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Line baking sheets with parchment paper.
Scoop rounded tablespoons of dough onto prepared sheets, spacing 2 inches apart. Sprinkle with flaky sea salt.
Bake 10–12 minutes until edges are golden but centers still look slightly underdone. They firm up as they cool.
Cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes before transferring to a wire rack.

notes: Don't overbake! The cookies will look underdone when you pull them out — that's the secret to the perfect chewy texture.
